  • View page source

app.models package

Submodules

app.models.collection_model module

The module defines a SQLAlchemy model representing a collection entity, which defines the structure of the collection and its relationships to other entities.

class app.models.collection_model.Collection(user_id, is_locked, collection_name, collection_summary=None)[source]

Bases: Base

SQLAlchemy model representing a collection entity. This model defines the structure of a collection, including its attributes such as the collection name, summary, and locked status. It manages relationships with other entities such as the user who creates the collection and the documents associated with the collection. The class also includes functionality to track the locking of the collection and the number of documents contained within it. It provides an asynchronous method for serializing the collection’s data into a dictionary format.

collection_documents
collection_name
collection_summary
collection_user
created_date
documents_count
id
is_locked
locked_date
async to_dict()[source]

Converts the collection instance to a dictionary. The dictionary contains various attributes of the collection such as its ID, creation and update dates, locked status, name, summary, and the count of associated documents.

updated_date
user_id

app.models.comment_model module

The module defines an SQLAlchemy model for comments, which represent user comments on documents. It defines the structure of the comment entity and its relationships with users and documents.

class app.models.comment_model.Comment(user_id, document_id, comment_content)[source]

Bases: Base

SQLAlchemy model representing a comment.

comment_content
comment_document
comment_user
created_date
document_id
id
property is_locked: bool

Determines if the associated document is locked.

async to_dict()[source]

Converts the comment instance into a dictionary representation.

updated_date
user_id

app.models.datafile_model module

app.models.download_model module

The module defines a SQLAlchemy model representing a download entity. It tracks the details of document downloads, including the associated user, document, and revision. The model also includes relationships to other entities such as users, documents, and revisions.

class app.models.download_model.Download(user_id, document_id, revision_id)[source]

Bases: Base

SQLAlchemy model representing a download entity. It stores information about downloads of documents and their revisions by users. The model tracks the user who initiated the download, the document being downloaded, and the specific revision of the document, as well as providing relationships to related entities such as users, documents, and revisions.

created_date
document_id
download_document
download_revision
download_user
id
revision_id
async to_dict()[source]

Converts the download instance to a dictionary. The dictionary includes attributes such as the download ID, creation date, user ID and name, document ID, document filename, and revision ID.

user_id

app.models.favorite_model module

The module defines the Favorite model for managing user favorites of documents in the database. The Favorite class includes fields for tracking the user who favorited a document, and the date when the favorite was created. It also establishes relationships with User and document models and provides methods to initialize instances and convert them to dictionaries.

class app.models.favorite_model.Favorite(user_id, document_id)[source]

Bases: Base

Represents a user’s favorite document. Each favorite is associated with a specific user and document, including a creation timestamp. The model tracks the relationship between users and the documents they mark as favorites and provides methods for data serialization.

created_date
document_id
favorite_document
favorite_user
id
async to_dict()[source]

Converts the Favorite instance into a dictionary representation. This method returns the favorite’s ID, creation date, user ID, document ID, and the dictionary representation of the associated document.

user_id

app.models.option_model module

The module defines the SQLAlchemy model for the option entity, which is used to store and manage configuration values for both the application and its plugins within the database.

class app.models.option_model.Option(user_id, option_key, option_value)[source]

Bases: Base

SQLAlchemy model for an option entity. The model is used to store and manage configuration values for both the application and its plugins within the database.

created_date
id
option_key
option_user
option_value
async to_dict()[source]

Converts the option instance to a dictionary representation, including all necessary attributes.

updated_date
user_id

app.models.revision_model module

The module defines an SQLAlchemy model for document revisions. It describes the structure of the revision entity, including its fields such as filename, size, and MIME type. The module also manages the relationships between revisions and other entities like users and documents. It includes functionality for handling encrypted thumbnail filenames and provides methods for deleting associated files when a revision is deleted.

class app.models.revision_model.Revision(user_id, document_id, revision_filename, revision_size, revision_mimetype, thumbnail_filename=None)[source]

Bases: Base

SQLAlchemy model representing a document revision. It contains metadata for each revision, such as the filename, size, MIME type, and associated user and document. The class also manages encrypted thumbnail filenames and provides properties for retrieving the thumbnail’s path and URL. The revision entity is linked to its corresponding document and user, and it includes relationships with other entities such as downloads and shards. Additionally, it ensures that associated files are deleted asynchronously when a revision is removed from the database.

created_date
document_id
id
revision_document
revision_downloads
revision_filename
revision_mimetype
revision_shards
revision_size
revision_user
property thumbnail_filename: str

Retrieves the decrypted thumbnail filename for the revision. Returns None if the thumbnail filename is not encrypted.

thumbnail_filename_encrypted
property thumbnail_path

Constructs and returns the file system path for the revision’s thumbnail.

property thumbnail_url

Constructs and returns the URL for the revision’s thumbnail.

async to_dict()[source]

Converts the revision instance into a dictionary representation, including relevant details like the revision filename, size, MIME type, and thumbnail URL.

user_id
app.models.revision_model.after_delete_listener(mapper, connection, revision: Revision)[source]

Event listener triggered after a revision entity is deleted. Schedules tasks to delete the revision’s main file and the associated thumbnail file, if one exists.

async app.models.revision_model.delete_revision(revision: Revision)[source]

Asynchronously deletes the main file associated with the specified revision entity. Logs any errors that occur during the deletion process.

async app.models.revision_model.delete_thumbnail(revision: Revision)[source]

Asynchronously deletes the thumbnail file associated with the specified revision entity. Logs any errors that occur during the deletion process.

app.models.tag_model module

This module defines the Tag model for associating tags with documents in the database. The Tag class includes fields for the tag value and the associated document ID, and provides methods for initializing instances of tags. The model supports the relationship with the Document model.

class app.models.tag_model.Tag(document_id, tag_value)[source]

Bases: Base

Represents a tag associated with a document. Each tag is linked to a specific document and contains a tag value. The class supports the relationship between documents and their associated tags, and provides methods for initializing tags for documents.

created_date
document_id
id
tag_document
tag_value

app.models.user_model module

The module defines the User model for managing users in the system. The User class includes fields for personal information, authentication details, and multi-factor authentication (MFA). It also provides methods to manage user roles, permissions, and security-related features. The class establishes relationships with other models like documents, collections, and downloads.

class app.models.user_model.User(user_role, user_login, user_password, first_name, last_name, is_active=False, user_caption='', user_contacts='')[source]

Bases: Base

The User class represents a user in the system. It stores personal information, authentication details, and multi-factor authentication (MFA) data. The class also manages roles, permissions, and relationships with other system models.

property can_admin: bool

Checks if the user has administrative permissions. Returns True if the user role is ‘admin’, False otherwise.

property can_edit: bool

Checks if the user has editing permissions. Returns True if the user role is ‘admin’ or ‘editor’, False otherwise.

property can_read: bool

Checks if the user has read permissions. Returns True if the user role is ‘admin’, ‘editor’, ‘writer’, or ‘reader’, False otherwise.

property can_write: bool

Checks if the user has write permissions. Returns True if the user role is ‘admin’, ‘editor’, or ‘writer’, False otherwise.

created_date
first_name
full_name

Returns the full name of the user (first name and last name).

id
is_active
property jti: str

Retrieves the decrypted JWT identifier (JTI). Returns the decrypted JWT identifier.

jti_encrypted
last_login_date
last_name
mfa_attempts
property mfa_secret: str

Retrieves the decrypted multi-factor authentication (MFA) secret. Returns the decrypted MFA secret.

mfa_secret_encrypted
property mfa_url

Generates the URL for setting up MFA for the user. Returns the URL that can be used for MFA setup for the user.

password_accepted
password_attempts
password_hash
suspended_date
async to_dict() → dict[source]

Converts the user object to a dictionary representation. Returns a dictionary with user details like id, login, role, etc.

updated_date
user_caption
user_collections
user_comments
user_contacts
user_documents
user_downloads
user_favorites
user_login
user_options
user_partners
user_revisions
user_role
user_shards
property user_totp

Generates a one-time password (OTP) for multi-factor authentication. Returns the current OTP generated for MFA using the user’s secret.

property userpic_filename: str

Retrieves the decrypted user profile picture filename. Returns the decrypted filename of the user’s profile picture, or None if not set.

userpic_filename_encrypted
property userpic_path

Retrieves the file path of the user’s profile picture. Returns the file path of the user’s profile picture if available.

property userpic_url

Retrieves the URL of the user’s profile picture. Returns the URL of the user’s profile picture if available.

class app.models.user_model.UserRole(value)[source]

Bases: Enum

An enumeration representing the possible roles a user can have. These roles determine the level of access and permissions a user has in the system.

admin = 'admin'
editor = 'editor'
reader = 'reader'
writer = 'writer'
